content.problemStatement:
A positive integer NвЙ•1N\ge 1NвЙ•1 is given.
An NNN-uple (x1,x2,вА¶,xN)(x_1, x_2, \dots, x_N)(x1вАЛ,x2вАЛ,вА¶,xNвАЛ) of nonnegative integers is special if, after we remove all entries which are zero, its entries are nondecreasing. For example (0,2,0,0,4,0,4,5,0,0)(0, 2, 0, 0, 4, 0, 4, 5, 0, 0)(0,2,0,0,4,0,4,5,0,0) is special, but (3,2,0)(3, 2, 0)(3,2,0) and (0,1,3,0,0,1,0)(0, 1, 3, 0, 0, 1, 0)(0,1,3,0,0,1,0) are not.

One can show that there is a unique function ќЉ\muќЉ defined on the NNN-uples of nonnegative integers such that, for any NNN-uple x=(x1,x2,вА¶,xN)x=(x_1, x_2,\dots, x_N)x=(x1вАЛ,x2вАЛ,вА¶,xNвАЛ)

вИСyвЙ§xќЉ(y)={1if x is special0otherwise,\sum_{y \le x} \mu(y) =
\begin{cases}
    1 &\text{if $x$ is special} \\
    0 &\text{otherwise}
\end{cases},yвЙ§xвИСвАЛќЉ(y)={10вАЛif x is specialotherwiseвАЛ,where the sum ranges over all NNN-uples y=(y1,y2,вА¶,yN)y=(y_1, y_2,\dots, y_N)y=(y1вАЛ,y2вАЛ,вА¶,yNвАЛ) of nonnegative integers such that yiвЙ§xiy_i\le x_iyiвАЛвЙ§xiвАЛ for all 1вЙ§iвЙ§N1\le i\le N1вЙ§iвЙ§N.

You are given N,MвЙ•1N, M \ge 1N,MвЙ•1. Compute the remainder modulo 998вАЙ244вАЙ353998\,244\,353998244353 of the number of NNN-uples x=(x1,x2,вА¶,xN)x=(x_1,x_2,\dots, x_N)x=(x1вАЛ,x2вАЛ,вА¶,xNвАЛ) such that 0вЙ§xiвЙ§M0\le x_i\le M0вЙ§xiвАЛвЙ§M for all 1вЙ§iвЙ§N1\le i\le N1вЙ§iвЙ§N and ќЉ(x)=0\mu(x) = 0ќЉ(x)=0.


Input Format

The only line contains two positive integers NNN, MMM, the size of the tuples and the maximum possible value of the entries of the tuples.


Output Format
Print the the number of NNN-uples x=(x1,x2,вА¶,xN)x=(x_1,x_2,\dots, x_N)x=(x1вАЛ,x2вАЛ,вА¶,xNвАЛ) such that 0вЙ§xiвЙ§M0\le x_i\le M0вЙ§xiвАЛвЙ§M for all 1вЙ§iвЙ§N1\le i\le N1вЙ§iвЙ§N and ќЉ(x)=0\mu(x) = 0ќЉ(x)=0. As it can be large, print it modulo 998вАЙ244вАЙ353998\,244\,353998244353.
